mnemonics.title: Big O Complexity Order
mnemonics.content:
C O N S T A N T - O(1)
L O G - O(log n)
N - O(n)
N L O G - O(n log n)
Q U A D R A T I C - O(n²)
C U B I C - O(n³)
E X P O N E N T I A L - O(2^n)

mnemonics.title: Tree Traversals
mnemonics.content:
Preorder: Root Left Right (R-L-R)
Inorder: Left Root Right (L-R-R)
Postorder: Left Right Root (L-R-R)
Mnemonic: 'R' visits Root; positions change for L and R positions.

mnemonics.title: Stack Operations
mnemonics.content:
PUSH - Push It In
POP - Pull It Out
PEEK - See Top Without Removing
Mnemonic: Push to add at TOP, Pop to remove from TOP.

acronyms.usage: Level-by-level exploration of graph using queue
Deep exploration of graph using stack or recursion
Fundamental topic for efficient problem solving
Principle used by Stack data structure
Principle used by Queue data structure
Hierarchical data structure for efficient searching

visualTips.visualization: Draw a graph with Y-axis (time) and X-axis (input size). Show curves: flat line for O(1), slowly rising for O(log n), linear for O(n), steeper for O(n²). The steeper the curve, worse the performance.

Stack: Draw a vertical container with items stacked. Adding/removes from top only.
Queue: Draw a horizontal pipe with entrance (rear) and exit (front). Items move forward.
Draw an inverted tree. Root at top, branches downward. Level 0 has 1 node, Level 1 has 2 nodes, Level 2 has 4 nodes. Pattern: 2^level nodes.
Draw Russian nesting dolls. Each doll contains smaller version of itself until base case (smallest doll) is reached.

analogies.analogy: A recipe for cooking - step-by-step instructions to achieve desired output from given ingredients.
Different containers for storage - fridge for groceries, bookshelf for books, locker for valuables.
Speed of a car - faster cars (lower complexity) reach destination quicker than slower ones.
Library catalog system - each book (key) has a specific shelf location (hash value) for quick retrieval.
Mirrors facing each other - infinite reflection showing same pattern repeating at smaller scales.
Exploring a maze - BFS like sending explorers in all directions simultaneously, DFS like following one path completely before backtracking.
